lede: A look at July 3 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Nefisa Berberović and Corey Sevier, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: President of Egypt
events.desc: President of Egypt Mohamed Morsi is removed from office by the military after four days of protests all over the country calling for his resignation, to which he did not respond. The president of the Supreme Constitutional Court of Egypt, Adly Mansour, is declared acting president until further elections are held.

events.title: United States Navy
events.desc: United States Navy warship USS Vincennes shoots down Iran Air Flight 655 over the Persian Gulf, killing all 290 people aboard.

events.title: Jimmy Carter
events.desc: U.S. President Jimmy Carter signs the first directive for secret aid to the opponents of the pro-Soviet regime in Kabul.

events.title: David Bowie
events.desc: David Bowie retires his stage persona Ziggy Stardust with the surprise announcement that it is "the last show that we'll ever do" on the last day of the Ziggy Stardust Tour.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: The "Falls Curfew" begins in Belfast, Northern Ireland.

events.title: Dan-Air Flight 1903
events.desc: Dan-Air Flight 1903 crashes into the Les Agudes mountain in the Montseny Massif near the village of Arbúcies in Catalonia, Spain, killing all 112 people aboard.

events.title: SS United States
events.desc: The SS United States sets sail on her maiden voyage to Southampton. During the voyage, the ship takes the Blue Riband away from the RMS Queen Mary.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Royal Navy attacks the French naval squadron in Algeria, to ensure that it will not fall under German control. Of the four French battleships present, one is sunk, two are damaged, and one escapes back to France.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: George Washington takes command of the Continental Army at Cambridge, Massachusetts.

events.title: French and Indian War
events.desc: French and Indian War: George Washington surrenders Fort Necessity to French forces.

births.name: Joanne Herring
births.line: American socialite, businesswoman, political activist, philanthropist, diplomat, and television talk show host

births.name: Jean Collas
births.line: French rugby player and tug of war competitor (died 1928)

deaths.text: Lew Hoad, Australian tennis player and coach
Reginald Marsh (artist), French-American painter, illustrator, and academic
